title: Will sharp elbows and financing concerns force investors to rotate out of the AI theme?

Happy Monday out there, team 42. It's your skipper here, Darius Dale, to present our macro minute from Monday, June 29th, 2026. As always, we'll start with the executive summary from today's lead-off morning note, so let's dive right in. Today's uh key macro question is, will sharp elbows and financing concerns force investors to rotate out of the AI theme? The short answer is the risk of real-world constraints on compute capacity and growing scrutiny over circular financing and historic demands for capital may force investors to curtail their exposure to one of, if not the most crowded trades in modern financial market history. To try to add a little nuance, the BIS flagged risks to global prosperity from an AI bust, inflation, and fiscal stress. The BIS cited pressure points demanding attention, warning that financial vulnerabilities could amplify shocks as fiscal space is constrained. The BIS also flagged funding risks like circular financing deals, plus threats to financial conditions from resurgent inflation and high sovereign debt. Quote, "Disappointment in returns could trigger a sudden pullback in financing and turn the CapEx boom into a protracted investment bust with potential knock-on effects on finance financial conditions," the BIS said, adding that a quote, "Major equity market correction could have larger macroeconomic consequences today than in the past." Something we agree with. Uh Basel officials uh said uh other assets face similar risks, especially credit. Repricing of risk this time, whether triggered by higher interest rates or an AI bust, has a has the potential to be similarly disruptive in credit on par with 2008's global financial crisis, the BIS said. These warnings come amid fresh evidence that the AI boom is straining compute and the needed in energy needed to power proliferating data centers. Uh the latest news on this front is Google curbing curbing Gemini AI model access for Meta Platforms after failing to supply enough compute. In the latest sign of AI infrastructure strain, Alphabet's search unit restricted several clients, hitting Meta the hardest. The curbs hit Meta's internal projects and pushed it to tell staff to economize AI tokens. This follows Google recently agreeing to pay SpaceX $920 million monthly for compute in a $30 billion deal running through mid-2029. With these recent curbs, Google may be seeking to establish a higher price floor that is more aligned with its upward sloping cost curve. As always, I will wrap up with the question from our community. This one's titled a Dr. Mo Tactical Execution System uh question. Says a quick question quick Dr. Mo question for our community. I'm curious to learn what processes others use to make Dr. Mo uh driven tactical trades in their portfolio. I understand how Dr. Mo works from a proper trade perspective, but I'm also curious to how people execute within their own portfolios. Which asset styles do you use and how do you uh choose which ones to use? Do you adjust styles for the regime? Do you strictly adhere to uh the proper trade signals or use them as a guide? Currently, I'm grabbing uh I'm grabbing on tight to KISS. Just follow KISS. Are great words to live by and it's great in all caps. But, I'm interested in how others use Dr. Mo from a portfolio management uh standpoint. So, uh excellent question. This is something that I'm extremely passionate about. You know, one of the things we built here at 42 Macro is not just a research firm and and and a valuable research partner, but we've also built uh what we consider to be an outsourced risk manager risk management uh firm for thousands of assets around the world whether they be institutional or retail. Uh and the whole point of KISS is to simplify all the risk management signals and tools that we use into a very simple, you know, kind of easy to execute portfolio. When it's fully invested, KISS is 60% stocks, 30% gold, 10% Bitcoin. Obviously, the KISS has systematic risk management overlays that I've talked about here and in other programs uh that guide that exposure up and down based on the market regime on terms of the ball targeting based on how the assets themselves are performing in terms of the dynamic position sizing. Dr. Mo does that for 70 different factors across uh equities, uh fixed income, credit, uh, macro exposures including currencies, commodities, crypto, uh, etc. And so, uh, what the question is essentially asking is, you know, for investors who are not necessarily following KISS with their entire portfolio, how are they incorporating uh, our Dr. Mo signals to produce better outcomes for their for their portfolio. So, let me just walk you through a particular uh, an example today. You know, if you look at our KISS portfolio, I don't usually do this, but I you know, in terms of answering this question, I think it's worth uh, highlighting. You know, you think about KISS right now, KISS is currently uh, at 60% uh, uh, stocks, 40% cash. It's at 100% of its maximum exposure of 60% in stocks. That's a that's an important signal. It's at 100% of its maximum exposure of 0% uh, in uh, uh, 0% of its maximum exposure of 30% in gold, 0% of its maximum exposure of 10% Bitcoin. So, uh, one of the ways in which I've seen investors use uh, both here in our community, but also among our pro-to-pro uh, clients, um, you know, with and our pro-to-pro clients typically span uh, hedge funds, uh, uh, family offices, uh, investment advisors, you know, folks who you know, have the budget for my time. Um, and so, when I'm having conversations with them about, you know, how do we take these signals and create better outcomes for our clients, you know, one of the things that I've seen that's pretty common is in risk-on market regimes, you know, to the extent that gold uh, is uh, you know, not not fully invested or Bitcoin is not fully invested as they currently are and have been for quite a while. Recall that we got out of Bitcoin uh, last fall and we sold gold in two tranches uh, over the course of the spring of this year. And so, uh, you know, when we're in a risk-on market regime as we are currently, we're currently in a risk-on reflation market regime, investors will instead of, you know, allocating that 40% of cash, they'll maybe allocate more to stocks, maybe take that up to 60 or even 80% and then allocate the rest to cash or allocate the rest to uh, uh, uh, fixed income instrument that is more consistent with the risk-on market regime like uh, I don't know, for example, leveraged loans, BKLN, uh, uh, high yield credit, uh, investment grade credit, uh, you know, emerging market bonds, you know, things that you'll you'll pick up that extra yield. You know, it's ultimately what a risk-on market regime is telling you is that, you know, the the the markets you you generally want to get you generally want to extend yourself out on the risk curve during a risk-on market regime cuz the market is paying you to take risk. When you transition to a risk-off market regime, which is inflation or deflation, basically you want to be doing the exact opposite of what I just said in terms of increasing the amount of risk on your exposure in your portfolio. So, I raising more cash, you know, getting out of the riskier parts of the fixed income and credit markets and more close to closely aligned with T-bills and money market fund exposure for the cash exposure. And obviously if we went to a risk-on market regime, the equity exposure in KISS would take itself down uh from from 100% of its maximum exposure to something like 50% or even 25% or zero uh percent like gold and Bitcoin currently are. So, uh those are um, you know, great examples of of how uh I've seen investors double within our community and also among our Portfolio uh clients, um, you know, use our system. Uh you know, another way uh we've seen them use our system is, you know, we we use VT, the Vanguard Total World Stock ETF, uh for the gross exposure in for the the equity exposure in KISS. You know, investors might want to take more risk than that. So, let's say maybe they go half VT, uh you know, half SMH semiconductors, uh maybe half, you know, I'm jump you know, I'm I'm I've I've seen that joke joking. That's pretty uh that's pretty uh that's pretty high beta. But, uh you know, maybe you go uh you want some tech exposure, you want more exposure to a particular thing like AI, you want less exposure to a particular thing like AI. You know, those are all kinds of choices that we allow investors to make uh using our discretionary risk management overlay, you know, and the signals the proper trade uh signals for, you know, things like technology, things like mega cap uh growth, etc. The types of exposures that we have uh that are exposures to the AI theme. And and this is just, you know, I'm obviously focusing on AI because that's the most common and crowded theme in in financial markets right now. But again, this you know, this is the same the same tools that will allow you to get more or less exposure to the AI theme or the same exact tools that will allow you to get more or less exposure to subsequent themes in future market cycles. And so, ultimately the key takeaway is that, you know, KISS is obviously designed to be, you know, very simple and effective and it is very effective as this you know, client member is highlighting, but to the extent that you want to produce different results in your portfolio, you want to take a different level of risk than the standard KISS portfolio, it's very easy to do that with the signals that we produce with Dr. Mo across equity sectors, equity factors, global equities, fixed income sectors, and macro exposures. So, this is a great thread kind of highlighting how different members use those Dr. Mo signals to create customized versions of KISS that are more suitable for their investment purposes and risk tolerance and strategic investment objectives all in ways that are helping them achieve financial freedom and that's something I'm very proud of, we're very proud of here at the 42 macro team. So, we're wrapping up there. Terry Stahler with today's macro minute for Monday, June 29th, 2026. Best of luck out there today. We'll catch you back here tomorrow. Cheers.
